title: The Red Horizon

quoteLast: she slipped away to her own room and took off the dainty gown she had put on for Sandy's coming.
title: Sandy

quoteFirst: To the blythe moon he may not bear The maid, whose cheeks the daylight wear-- She fears to answer to his call
quoteLast: , underneath yon wall-- Nor can he find a birchen bower To screen her in the morning hour; And thus the summer days are fleeting Away, without the lovers meeting!
title: The Poetry of Wales
author: Jenkins, John, 1821-1896 [Editor]

quoteFirst: So home to dinner at 4 o’clock, and then to the office, and there late, and so home to supper and to bed, having sat up till past
quoteLast: to look over the account of the collections for the Fishery, and the loose and base manner that monies so collected are disposed of in, would make a man never part with a penny in that manner, and, above all, the inconvenience of having a great man, though never so seeming pious as my Lord Pembroke is.
title: The Diary of Samuel Pepys — Complete
author: Pepys, Samuel, 1633-1703; Braybrooke, Richard Griffin, Baron, 1783-1858 [Editor]; Wheatley, Henry B. (Henry Benjamin), 1838-1917 [Editor]; Bright, Mynors, 1818-1883 [Translator]

quoteFirst: He did not know how long these informal affairs were apt to last, but at a rough guess figured that they would still be on hand
quoteLast: and he ought to be there before that.
title: Joe Leslie's Wife; or, a Skeleton in the Closet
author: Robertson, Alexander, M.D., 1854-1938

quoteFirst: The old house at Armboth, on Thirlmere, has also the reputation of     being occasionally
quoteLast: supernaturally lighted up for the     reception of spectres, which cross the lake from Helvellyn for some     mysterious purpose within its walls.
title:

title: Slow and Sure: The Story of Paul Hoffman the Young Street-Merchant

quoteFirst: "The first thing," resumed Arthur, "is to plant a straight stick upright in the ground; when it casts no shadow east or west it is
quoteLast: _here_.  My watch will then show what time it is at the Kingsmills: if it shows an earlier hour there, we must be east of them; if a later hour, then we are west of them."
title: The Island Home
author: Archer, Richard, -1883; Dalziel, Edward, 1817-1905 [Illustrator]; Dalziel, George, 1815-1902 [Illustrator]
